mission.title: thanos: Deduplication returning deduped and non-deduped results in 0.31.0+
mission.description: Deduplication returning deduped and non-deduped results in 0.31.0+. This issue affects 10+ users.

steps.title: Identify thanos troubleshoot symptoms
steps.description:
Check for the issue in your thanos deployment:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n thanos -l app.kubernetes.io/name=thanos
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=thanos -n thanos --tail=100 | grep -i error
```
Look for errors or warnings in the logs that may indicate the issue.

steps.title: Review thanos configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant thanos configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n thanos -l app.kubernetes.io/name=thanos
kubectl get configmap -n thanos -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=thanos
```
**Thanos, Prometheus and Golang version used**:

Thanos Version: 0.31.0
Prometheus Version: 2.42.0

**Object Storage Provider**:

S3

**What happened**:

Slack Thread: https://cloud-native.slack.com/archives/CK5RSSC10/p1680534545839619

We recently

steps.title: Apply the fix for Deduplication returning deduped and non-deduped results in…
steps.description:
I believe this comes from the stores not re-ordering the series after adding the external labels. You can replicate it by adding the metrics:
```
metric{}
metric{alocal="label"}
```

And an external label `ext="label"`. Before the external label, `metric{} < metric{alocal="label"}` because it has
```yaml
- --query.replica-label=replica
            - --query.replica-label=prometheus_replica
            - --query.replica-label=cluster
            - --query.replica-label=__replica__
            - --query.replica-label=tenant_id
            - --query.replica-label=receive
            - --query.replica-label=prometheus
            - --query.replica-label=thanos_ruler_replica
            - --query.auto-downsampling
            - --query.max-concurrent-select=25
            - --store.response-timeout=1m
            - --query.max-concurrent=500
            - --query.metadata.default-time-range=6h
```

steps.title: Confirm Deduplication returning deduped and non-deduped… is resolved
steps.description:
Verify the fix by checking that the original error no longer occurs:
```bash
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=thanos -n thanos --tail=50 --since=5m
kubectl get events -n thanos --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm that the issue symptoms are gone.

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with Thanos deployed (sidecar, query, store gateway, compactor as needed). The thanos binary provides subcommands for each component.
